items.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
items.authors: Ruozhen He, Meng Wei, Ziyan Yang et al.
items.abstract: Multi-shot video generation extends single-shot generation to coherent visual narratives, yet maintaining consistent characters, objects, and locations across shots remains a challenge over long sequences. Existing evaluations typically use independently generated prompt sets with limited entity cov...

items.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
items.authors: Ziyu Guo, Rain Liu, Xinyan Chen et al.
items.abstract: Visual reasoning, often interleaved with intermediate visual states, has emerged as a promising direction in the field. A straightforward approach is to directly generate images via unified models during reasoning, but this is computationally expensive and architecturally non-trivial. Recent alterna...

items.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
items.authors: Shashwat Goel, Nikhil Chandak, Arvindh Arun et al.
items.abstract: AI agents are being increasingly deployed in dynamic, open-ended environments that require adapting to new information as it arrives. To efficiently measure this capability for realistic use-cases, we propose building grounded simulations that replay real-world events in the order they occurred. We ...

items.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
items.authors: Xiang Fan, Yuheng Wang, Bohan Fang et al.
items.abstract: Video generation powers a vast array of downstream applications. However, while the de facto standard, i.e., latent diffusion models, typically employ heavily conditioned denoising networks, their decoders often remain unconditional. We observe that this architectural asymmetry leads to significant ...

items.title: Articraft: An Agentic System for Scalable Articulated 3D Asset Generation
items.authors: Matt Zhou, Ruining Li, Xiaoyang Lyu et al.
items.abstract: A bottleneck in learning to understand articulated 3D objects is the lack of large and diverse datasets. In this paper, we propose to leverage large language models (LLMs) to close this gap and generate articulated assets at scale. We reduce the problem of generating an articulated 3D asset to that ...

items.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
items.authors: Zhuohang Li, Liqun Huang, Wei Xu et al.
items.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are prone to compounding errors in dexterous manipulation, where high-dimensional action spaces and contact-rich dynamics amplify small policy deviations over long horizons. While Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) can refine policies through human takeover data...
